split.rationale: Criminal law is a city-wide high-urgency service. Searchers in distress use '[offence] lawyer melbourne' not '[offence] lawyer [suburb]'. However, suburb-level pages near major Magistrates Courts (Dandenong, Ringwood, Sunshine, Broadmeadows) add genuine geographic authority and capture court-specific searches. 70/30 split maximises commercial return while building suburban presence near key court locations.

tier_a.rationale: Highest urgency + highest search volume + highest CPC in legal vertical

tier_b.rationale: Good commercial volume, strong intent signals, competitive but winnable

tier_c.rationale: Lower volume but genuine services — worthwhile as secondary keywords or content sections

tier_d.rationale: Homicide: MIXED SERP (informational), very low search volume for 'homicide lawyer'. Proceeds of crime, anti-semitic offences, tobacco importation: extremely low volume and niche. All covered as content on criminal defence service page.

location_pages.rationale: Pages near major Magistrates Court locations. Criminal matters are tried at the local Magistrates Court for the accused's area — a firm that appears in searches for '[suburb] criminal lawyer' captures clients who need court-local representation. Suburb selection = courts with highest case volumes.

suburbs.court: Dandenong Magistrates Court — one of the busiest courts in Victoria
Ringwood Magistrates Court — major outer-east court
Sunshine Magistrates Court — major western suburbs court
Broadmeadows Magistrates Court — major northern suburbs court
Frankston Magistrates Court — major south-east court
Near Sunshine/Werribee court cluster — western growth area

notes: New site (DA ~0) — prioritise achievable keywords first (medium competition) while building toward high-competition umbrella terms
Homepage targeting 'criminal lawyer melbourne' is correct as primary — this is the umbrella term all users search regardless of specific charge
Homicide excluded from keyword targets — MIXED SERP, low volume, cover as content on criminal defence page
Drug offences combined on one page (possession + trafficking) — same searcher intent, same service, SERP shows firms do this
Intervention orders is a strong separate category — enough volume to justify its own page
